title: Remdesivir effectively inhibits the recently emerged novel coronavirus (2019-nCoV) in vitro
abstract: We report that Remdesivir and Chloroquine are highly effective in the control of 2019-nCoV infection in vitro. Patients present with Fever, Cough, and Dyspnea. Their targets include RNA polymerase and ACE2 interactions.

title: Dexamethasone in Hospitalized Patients with Covid-19
abstract: In patients hospitalized with Covid-19, the use of Dexamethasone resulted in lower mortality. The drug acts by suppressing inflammatory pathways involving IL-6 and TNF-alpha, reducing Inflammation and Cytokine Storm.

title: Baricitinib plus Remdesivir for Hospitalized Adults with Covid-19
abstract: Baricitinib plus Remdesivir was superior to Remdesivir alone. Baricitinib specifically targets JAK1 and JAK2 to treat Respiratory Distress and systemic inflammation.

title: Donepezil and Memantine combination in Alzheimer's disease
abstract: The combination of Donepezil and Memantine is effective for Alzheimer's disease progression, addressing Cognitive Decline and Memory Loss. Donepezil targets AChE while Memantine is an NMDA receptor antagonist.

title: Metformin as a primary agent for Type 2 Diabetes
abstract: Metformin remains the primary pharmacological agent for managing Type 2 Diabetes. It helps manage Fatigue and high glucose levels. It acts via the activation of AMPK.

title: GLP-1 receptor agonists in Alzheimer's disease pathology
abstract: Liraglutide, typically used for Diabetes targeting GLP-1, shows neuroprotective effects in Alzheimer's disease models by modulating AMPK and reducing Cognitive Decline.

title: Tocilizumab in patients hospitalized with infectious SARS-CoV-2
abstract: Tocilizumab improves outcomes in patients with severe Covid-19. It acts as an antagonist to the IL-6 receptor, reducing the Cytokine Storm and Fever.

title: Repurposing potential of Metformin in COVID-19 management
abstract: Metformin activation of AMPK has been shown to reduce viral entry and inflammation in COVID-19 patients presenting with severe Respiratory Distress.

title: Donepezil effects on Type 2 Diabetes induced neuropathy
abstract: Donepezil modulation of AChE shows unexpected benefits in peripheral insulin sensitivity and Neuropathy in diabetic models.

title: Baricitinib for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus and similarities to COVID-19
abstract: Baricitinib inhibition of JAK1/2 targets multiple systemic diseases involving cytokines like IL-6 and symptoms like Pain and Fatigue.

title: Management of Common Cold and Viral Symptoms
abstract: The Common Cold is characterized by Cold, Fever, and Cough. Over-the-counter medications and anti-inflammatories are used for symptom relief.

title: Metformin as a potential anti-cancer agent in multiple cancer types
abstract: Metformin activation of AMPK exhibits anti-proliferative effects in cancer cells. Multiple cancer types show reduced Cell Proliferation and tumor growth when treated with Metformin.

title: Baricitinib ameliorates cancer-related inflammation and immunotherapy response
abstract: JAK1/JAK2 inhibition through Baricitinib enhances anti-tumor immunity in cancer models. The drug reduces Inflammation and improves therapeutic outcomes.

title: Repurposing Ibuprofen as an adjuvant in cancer therapy
abstract: NSAIDs like Ibuprofen have been shown to reduce cancer cell proliferation through COX-2 inhibition. Combination therapy shows improved outcomes in multiple cancer types.

title: Dexamethasone reduces cancer-related inflammation
abstract: Dexamethasone suppresses inflammatory cytokines IL-6 and TNF-alpha in cancer patients, reducing tumor-related Inflammation and Fever.
